This great museum captures the spirit of San José starting with the sculpture out front. The museum highlights local artists and the world of international artists who complete their works. I loved the painting and 3 D works 
The museum offers two small outdoor works each in a separate area to enjoy then in natural environment. 
The museum offers an interactive section and my favorite was the magnets on a moving plate. 
The ambiance is relaxed with no background noise just the conversations of the museumgoers 
Accessible entry vía ramp with entry and several steps 
Venue is accessible with wide open areas and elevator access.  Street parking around nearby Cesar Chávez park is paid by app or credit card or coins, some spots with 30 minute access and some with 2 hour limit.  Parking garage also available

this is a great art museum...enjoyed it thoroughly...lots to see...beautiful old historic building...huge gift shop...cute little cafe with fun coffees   ...will come again for rotating presentations...

I have walked by the San Jose Museum of Art many times. Since I had some time, I thought I should check it out. I agree with the others that it's not as big as it should be, and on my visit, they were changing the first floor exhibit, so only the second floor was open. There were some elementary school students taking a tour and they were very curious. I'm glad they are getting this kind of experience.

She: Are you a student or teacher?
Me: Yes, I am a teacher.

Scored free entry which was really nice. The work of Kambui Olujimi was very interesting! There was even a video he created about his fascination with gravity. There was also an exhibit called Still in Motion, with various artists. I was there for about 45 minutes. There were some pieces that I gravitated to, and the work was interesting.  Sometimes the large museums can be overwhelming without really absorbing the work.

I walked around the gift shop but didn't buy anything. There's also a cafe which I didn't partake in. Again, it's a great space!
